name: Ausrine
description: Ausrine, also known as Aušrinė, is the Lithuanian goddess of the dawn. She is closely associated with the morning star, Venus, and represents the beginning of a new day, hope, and youthful beauty. Ausrine is often depicted as a beautiful young woman, full of light and radiance, heralding the arrival of the sun goddess Saule. She is considered Saule's daughter or sister, depending on the account, and is responsible for preparing the way for her mother's daily journey across the sky. Ausrine's role is crucial in the cosmic order, as she ensures the cyclical renewal of life and the triumph of light over darkness. She is a symbol of purity, new beginnings, and the promise of a bright future. Her worship involved rituals performed at dawn, seeking blessings for fertility, health, and success. Ausrine also features in various folk tales and songs, emphasizing her connection to nature, beauty, and the rhythms of the natural world. She embodies the eternal cycle of birth, death, and rebirth, representing the enduring power of hope and renewal.
subtitle: Goddess of the Dawn, Morning Star

keyMyths.title: Ausrine and Saule's Journey
keyMyths.description: Ausrine prepares the path for Saule each morning, ensuring a safe and beautiful journey across the sky. She represents the promise of a new day.

keyMyths.title: Ausrine's Beauty
keyMyths.description: Ausrine's beauty is said to rival that of any mortal woman. Her presence brings light and joy to the world, inspiring hope and wonder.

keyMyths.title: Ausrine's Blessings
keyMyths.description: Worshippers would pray to Ausrine for blessings of fertility and good health, especially at dawn, hoping for a prosperous new day.

sources.author: Gimbutas, Marija
sources.work: The Goddesses and Gods of Old Europe
